narration: You just got a raise, and your gut says wait — will this push me into a higher tax bracket and leave me with less? That fear is real, and it's costing workers thousands.
visual: Worried person silhouette under falling dollar signs. 'TAX BRACKET MYTH' springs in large on dark background. Question mark hovers over the person's head. Amber accents throughout.

narration: Most people believe once you cross a bracket line, all your income gets taxed at that higher rate. That's completely wrong — and it's one of the most expensive myths in personal finance.
visual: Gray thought bubble containing '22% ON ALL INCOME?' A large red X sweeps diagonally across it. 'WRONG' springs in huge and red below the bubble. Light background.

narration: Tax brackets are marginal. Your income gets taxed in separate layers. Only the dollars above each threshold pay the higher rate — everything below stays at the lower rate.
visual: Three horizontal bars grow left to right: green 10% bar, amber 12% bar, red 22% bar. A dashed arrow points to the 22% bar labeled 'your raise lands here — only this slice.' Dark background.

narration: Say your $5,000 raise falls entirely in the 22% bracket — worst case. That's $1,100 in taxes. You still pocket $3,900 more. You literally cannot take home less by earning more.
visual: A $5,000 bar splits into $3,900 green section labeled 'YOU KEEP' and $1,100 amber section labeled 'TAXES'. Both numbers count up. Key insight box springs in: 'you NEVER earn less by earning more.' Light background.

narration: Workers who avoid raises or bonuses because of this myth lose around $8,000 a year on average. That's $80,000 a decade — gone because of bad advice someone who was also wrong passed along.
visual: Giant '$8K' springs in red. A person silhouette refuses a money bag with an X mark. A counter ticks: 'Year 1 = $8,000... Year 10 = $80,000 lost'. Draining red bar. Dark background.

narration: You can never take home less by earning more. Take the raise, the bonus, the extra shift. Share this before someone you know says no to free money today.
visual: Happy person silhouette accepting a money bag. 'TAKE THE RAISE' in huge text springs in. Amber, green, and red confetti dots bounce in around the scene. Share CTA box at the bottom. Light background.
